Title: RE: Phone issues
Notes:
Call received from Nathan. Requested him to create a remote session the 3cx system. Once the session has been created then setup a new session group for client and install the myremote on the server. After logged into the server with client and investigate the call flow issue with him. During the investigation found the following.

  1. The PBX is running on a Windows 10 machine, which is now end-of-life (EOL).
    We recommend upgrading the operating system to Windows 11 or a supported Windows Server OS.
  2. Multiple devices were attempting to authenticate against the PBX, which caused the system to blacklist several IPs. We have removed those entries for now and will continue monitoring in case anything similar occurs again.
  3. There are pending updates for the 3CX application.
    We will schedule the maintenance tonight after 9:00 PM and will update you once the maintenance is completed.
  4. One 3CX service was stuck in a 'Starting' state, which may also have contributed to the inbound call issue. We rebooted the server to resolve this.

    5.  

    After that the call flow issue was fixed and they were able to make inbound and outbound calls.

Title: RE: Phone issues
Notes: Took a backup of the 3CX configuration before performing maintenance. Installed all pending updates to bring 3CX to the latest version, then rebooted the machine. Verified that all 3CX services were up and running properly after the reboot. Tested the call flow using a test extension for both inbound and outbound calls, and confirmed everything is working fine. email have been sent to Nathan regarding this.
